title: LMbox Agent Manifest
description: Schema for the manifest.yaml at the root of an LMbox agent project. See ADR-001 in the lmbox-cli repo for the design rationale.

apiVersion.description: Contract version. Bump to v2 only on breaking changes.

kind.description: Resource kind. Reserved for forward compatibility (AgentBundle, AgentTemplate).

slug.description: kebab-case identifier, 3 to 64 chars. Must be unique per vendor.

version.description: Semantic version (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH with optional prerelease suffix).

vendor.description: The author of the agent. 'lmbox' for first-party, partner slug otherwise.

display_name.description: Human-facing name shown in OWUI and the marketplace.

description.description: One-paragraph description of what this agent does.

primary.description: Name registered in LiteLLM (e.g. mistral-large-2, gemma3-27b, qwen3-32b).

fallback.description: Used if `primary` returns an error or is unavailable on the box.

system.description: Path to the system prompt file, relative to manifest.yaml.

output_format.description: Optional structured-output contract. When set, the runtime forces the LLM to emit JSON matching the declared schema (via the OpenAI-compatible response_format=json_schema parameter on backends that support it; falls back to prompt-only enforcement otherwise).

kind.description: `markdown` (default — free-form text) or `json_schema` (structured output, validated post-generation).

schema.description: JSON Schema describing the expected output shape. Required when kind=json_schema. The runtime validates the LLM output against this before returning to the caller — invalid output triggers a re-prompt up to 2 retries.

grounding.description: Source-grounding contract (Layer D). When mode != off, every source_id field in the agent's structured output must match a source_id actually returned by a tool call during the run. Catches the 'invented source' hallucination class that pure citation verification misses.

mode.description: strict = block the run on any unmatched source_id; warn = report only, do not block (rollout phase); off = disable enforcement entirely.

source_id_paths.description: Dotted paths into the JSON output where source_id fields live. `*` matches every element of an array. Example: 'precedents.*.source_id'.

name.description: snake_case identifier used by the LLM in function calls.

type.description: Execution backend. 'rag' = vector search, 'action' = write op, 'http' = REST call, 'shell' = sandboxed command.

source.description: Resource reference for rag/action tools (e.g. connectors.sharepoint.contracts).

description.description: Description shown to the LLM via the function-calling schema.

required.description: LMbox connector slugs that MUST be active on the box for this agent to install.

optional.description: Connectors that enhance the agent if present, but are not blocking.

pass_threshold.description: Fraction of golden cases that must pass for `lmbox agent test` to exit 0.

golden.description: Path to the JSONL file containing golden test cases.

owui_role.description: OWUI role group that can invoke this agent. Mapped to AD groups via Authentik.

audit.description: When true, every invocation produces an AuditLog entry in the chain.

rgpd_redact.description: PII tags to strip from input/output before any cloud LLM call. Local-model calls are exempt.

runtime_hints.description: Kernel-specific overrides. Keys are kernel names (openclaw, hermes, lmbox-native). Adapters ignore keys for other kernels.
